https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_continuous_success_en.pdf

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/media-library/general-information/continuous-improvement-and-innovation-with-a-ccoe.pdf

 

 

Section 7] Lessons Learned

(this section is an ongoing work in progress)

 
Embrace the Proactive Maintenance & Contractual Maintenance Periods CMP
 
For me this is actually part of the Cloud Mindset
 
Cloud Mindset - get everybody onboard
 
RISE Leadership Guide  - Everybody should read this https://www.sap.com/documents/2019/05/44b3ebd5-4b7d-0010-87a3-c30de2ffd8ff.html
 
S/4HANA Upgrades
 
Upgrades are recommended be done in a Project Dual Line Landscape - period
 
Releases and Upgrades should be clubbed together where possible
 
This will then...
 
Protect Production !!!
 
Protect the Program !!!
 
Program Team
 
Have a dedicated Team, don't have Operations People working on the S/4 Transformation part time as that can be a contributor to delays in the Program, and a Program wide delay of only 1 month will cover the cost of dedicated people
 
Other Non-SAP Teams in your Organisation
 
If the Program is depending on deliveries from other Non-SAP Teams in the Organisations, eg there will need to be a custom development on Service-Now or SalesForce or whatever other system then you need to make sure that those Teams have people/resource capacity, it has happened  that the whole S/4 Transformation Program is delayed in one of the Releases because of  Resource Capacity in a totally different Team which wasn't foreseen in the planning
 
Stick to the Plan !!!
 
Protect Production !!!
 
Protect the Program !!!
 
If it works don't fix it, and don't try to save money...
 
When the Program is running really well, don't try to cut corners and save money - if the Program is working don't fix it, because, you run the risk, that saving a few pennies here and there ends up leading to delays where x100's of consultants cannot work and the costs per month of the delay are thousands of times the amount that you saved by trying to cut corners and cut costs
 
If the Program is working and everything is going well, don't start trying to cut costs
 
FUE's
 
It is extremely important to take care of how you classify your Users, all Users, Dialog and System/Technical, because if you don't classify a User then that User will be measured as Advanced and charged accordingly.
 
Rotating Keys
 
TLS Certificiates
 
When you Rotate Keys in S/4HANA make sure that all dependent systems have the new keys, eg MBC Multi Bank Connectivity has the S/4HANA certificate and you need to update it there too

Don't skip "Phase 0", plan to give Phase Zero the time it needs

Key Users

Key Users are one of the keys to a successful GoLive make sure they are trained and have all of the support they need

 
Key Users are your Ambassadors, make sure they have everything they need
 
Investment and Multi-Year Multiple Release/GoLive Transformations
 
Everything is relative, whatever you can afford, make sure to invest in the success of the first GoLive/Release,do not cut costs or corners on the first GoLive/Release, the first GoLive/Release MUST be a SUCCESS.
 
Subsequent GoLives/Releases will in general be a rinse and repeat of the template for how
the first GoLive/Release was executed and you can reduce costs on those GoLives/Releases
 
S/4 Data Transformation and Integration
 
We need to do Mapping and Transformations of Data, either at the SAP End or the other End - this is another challenge to the "Clean-Core" and the S/4 Transformations, there needs to be a principle, mappings/transformation must happen outside of the S/4
 
Technical GoLive should be separated / disconnected from the Business GoLive
 
Technical GoLive several months before Business GoLive

RISE Proactive Maintenance for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Contractual Maintenance Periods

SAP and Customer agree regular periods where there can be downtimes for SAP to do Maintenance

Plan this in your Release Planning

S/4HANA Upgrades

In your multi-year Release Plan, make room for S/4HANA Upgrades to enable you to benefit from the latest SAP innovations

Capacity

You need to care for your Capacity and Data Volume Management

Basis Projects and Operations

You need to re-design your Project Basis Team and Operations Basis Team and methods of working according to the segregation of duties with the RISE ECS Team

Run SGEN straight after systems are delivered and before GoLives

Performance Tuning & House Keeping  - that's your job

FUE Licensing Costs and SAP Roles and Authorisations

SAP RISE S/4HANA usage is measured and sized in what’s called FUE’s, Full Usage Equivalents​, pay attention to this

SAP RISE AntiVirus

VSI is a extra service, not mandatory as of today

EWA Report

Pay very close to attention to it, it will become one of your best friends

S/4HANA FailOver and DR

Understand what you really get with 99.7 and 99.9 regarding Availability Zones and Regions

S/4HANA Preparation

Before installation, specify all Security Requirements and get them included in the installation and post installation

RISE is about Segregation of Duties on the Basis Team

RISE is about running Basis as a Factory with clear Roles and Responsibilities and Methods or Working

RISE is about Proper Planning an Preparation

Basis Trial and Error way of working evolves to Planning and Preparation way of working

 
Service Requests
 
Quality
 
Right First Time
 
SAP do not quality control Service Requests
 
Learn the Templates
 
RISE Technical Architecture
 
Is not the same as OnPremise
 
A RISE LoadBalancer or Application Gateway, does not necessarily work the same way on RISE as it does in your own DataCenter/HyperScaler even if they are the same
 
The SAP RISE Architecture View
 
Get from SAP as much technical architecture views of the RISE "Customer Setup" as possible
 
SAP Technical Architects are the an integral part of the new Basis Team
 
Basis Team benefits from being experienced and disciplined 
 
RISE Architect from SAP
 
Get a SAP RISE Architect from SAP onboarded to the project from the beginning

SAP CALM is your new best friend

Sizing and Performance Testing better in than out

Trust and Verify

There are very useful RISE lessons learned here from @shaji_narayanan 

Lessons Learned from SAP ERP Conversion to RISE wi... - SAP Community

 

And most of all, enjoy the RISE Journey 🛫

 

Andy Silvey.

SAP Technology Architect [Contractor]

Author Bio:

Andy Silvey is a 25 years SAP Technology veteran [15 years SAP Basis and 10 years SAP Tech Arch including Tech, Integration, Security, Data from 3.1H to S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP and everything in between, and former SCN Moderator and Mentor alumni].

entry.title: My Home in SAP S/4HANA - A Deep Dive as of 2408
entry.content:
My Home is your personalized entry page within SAP S/4HANA,  that provides you with easy access to the business insights, tasks, and applications you need to do your job with greater speed and agility. My Home lets you access and manage your to-dos, your favorite pages, your favorite apps, and your insights on any device. It builds on the functionality of the SAP Fiori launchpad and leverages Spaces and Pages (as a prerequisite). The new My Home was first released with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2208 release. Since its first release, there have been great improvements in its functionality and user experience. In this blog, we’ll revisit the features of My Home that will enhance your user experience and productivity.  

Video: Welcome to the My Home in SAP S/4HANA 

My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

 

 

My Home Sections 

 

The My Home entry page is divided into different sections. You can personalize the content of each section to fit your specific needs. 

 

To-Dos 

The To-Dos section provides an overview of your daily tasks. It combines your tasks from the My Inbox application and your situations from the standard and extended My Situations applications. The tasks display relevant business information about your assigned workflow items. It enables you to process your tasks without ever having to leave My Home. If Collaborative ERP is enabled on the system, you can collaborate directly with colleagues via MS Teams to resolve issues about your tasks quickly.  

Video: Managing the To-Dos Section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home To Do Section

 

 

 

News 

The News section keeps you updated on your company's latest news, or SAP S/4HANA system news. If SAP S/4HANA system news is displaying, you can shuffle through system news from different lines of business. You can manage SAP S/4HANA system news by choosing a line of business  that interests you to stay focused on relevant information. 

Video: Personalizing the News section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home News Section

 

 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home System News articles

 

 

 

Pages  

The Pages section provides you with quick access to your favorite or most used pages. You can manage the visibility of your pages and order them in a way that enhances your productivity.  

Video: Personalizing the Pages section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home Pages Section

 

 

 

Apps 

The Apps section provides easy access to your favorite, most used, recently used, and recommended applications. The Apps section improves the discoverability of your applications. The Recommended tab offers app suggestions using SAP Business AI, based on your user-specific role assignment. You can add apps to the Favorites tab from your most used, recently used, and recommended applications. You can organize and categorize your favorite applications by using the grouping, ordering and color-coding functionality so you can access them even more efficiently. 

Video: Personalizing the Apps section 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home Apps Section

 

 

 

Insights Tiles and Insights Cards 

The Insights section allows you to manage and monitor your company’s activities. It shows critical business information through KPIs and micro charts. This section is broken into two sections: Insights Tiles and Insights Cards. Insights cards and tiles display dynamic content at a glance. They provide quick and easy access to the applications that can provide more detailed information. Insights tiles offer analytical information, such as charts or KPIs. Insights cards are similar to Insights tiles but provide more detailed analytical information in the form of charts, tables or lists from supporting overview page-, list report-, and analytical list page-based applications. Thanks to this information, you can assess what is happening at any time and take action when needed.  You can customize the Insights section to display information that enables you to do your job with speed and agility. 

Video: Personalizing the Insights Tiles Section 

Video: Personalizing the Insights Cards Section 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home Insights Section

 

 

The My Home Layout and Advanced Settings 

 You can personalize the individual My Home sections, and another key feature is the ability to customize the layout of these sections on My Home. This personalization capability allows you to prioritize the information that is most important to you. You can manage the visibility and the display order of each of the My Home sections. 

Video: Personalizing the Layout of My Home  

Once you have personalized the My Home sections you can easily share your personalized content with co-workers who have the same or a similar role as you. This  enables them to also work with speed and agility. 

Video: Sharing the Personalized Content 

 

 

 

Now that we have given you a recap of the available My Home functionality in SAP S/4HANA, we hope you enjoy working with My Home in SAP S/4HANA and reap its benefits. You can  look forward to more enhancements that will improve your My Home experience even further in future releases of SAP S/4HANA.  

Note: Innovations appear first in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The demos in the videos were recorded on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in September 2024. 

Stay tuned! 

Caroline Welsh and Anna Freitag 

Product Managers of My Home, Cloud ERP UX Engineering.

entry.title: Service Order with Procurement of Spare Parts from Intercompany Supplier
entry.content:
In this blog you will learn about the Service Order process with Procurement of Spare Parts from Intercompany Supplier (Intercompany Stock Transport Order). This service process flow is based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, release 2023. 

 

Step 1: Create and Release Service Order with External Spare Part item 

Start the process by creating a service order with an External Spare Part item. Standard item category SRPM can be used. In the Involved Parties on item level you can enter a prefered vendor and enter an Intercompany Supplier. 

When releasing the service order (and save), a purchase requisition is created. The purchase requisition is visible in the Transaction History. 

In this example we first create a Purchase Requisition. It is also possible to configure the Service Order item category to immediately create a Purchase Order. 

 

Step 2: Convert Purchase Requisition into Purchase Order

In our example, company code 1710 is requesting this spare part from company code 1010 and plant 1010. A Supplier Business Partner is created for Plant 1010. 

The Purchase Order is created for this Intercompany Supplier and the Purchase Order is account assigned to the Service Order Item. 

Based on the Documentation described here, the Purchase Order type is NB, item category BLANK, and Delivery type NLCC. 

The Purchase Order is also visible in the Transaction History of the Service Order. 

 

Step 3: Outbound Delivery for Purchase Order

The Intercompany Supplier (Plant 1010) creates an Outbound Delivery based on the Purchase Order (transaction VL10B).

 

Step 4: Outbound Delivery - Perform Picking and Goods Issue

From the Outbound Delivery, perform picking of the spare parts in Plant 1010 and trigger Goods Issue. Standard movement type 643 is used in our example as a 2-step approach. After this goods movement, stock in the Receiving Company is updated as "Stock in Transit - Cross-Company".

A separate goods receipt needs to posted in the demanding company (1710). 

 

Step 5: Goods Receipt for Outbound Delivery

Create a Goods Receipt based on the Outbound Delivery (using movement type 101). 

 

Step 6: Check updates in Service Order

Both goods movements (good issue and goods receipt) are added to the Transaction History of the Service Order.

 

Step 7: Create Intercompany Invoice

The supplying company (1010) creates an intercompany invoice towards the requesting company (1710).

 

Step 8: Create and post Vendor Invoice 

In the Requesting Company, create and post the Vendor Invoice from the Intercompany supplier. The vendor invoice is added to the Purchase Order history.

 

Step 9: Check Margin Analysis for the Service Order

In Margin Analysis for the Service Order item we see the cost postings from the Goods Receipt and correction from the Vendor Invoice.

 

Step 10: Create Service Confirmation

Now that the Spare Part is available in the Requesting Company, the service technician can create a service confirmation and use the spare part.

 

Step 11: Create Customer Invoice

When the service is finished and the service order is completed, the customer billing process can be started.

 

Step 12: Margin Analysis

Finally, check revenue and cost postings in the Margin Analysis for the Service Order.

 

Variant: Service Order with External Part Procurement at Intercompany Supplier and direct shipment to customer

In the standard process, the spare parts are delivered by the intercompany supplier to the requesting company. It is also possible that the intercompany supplier directly ships the spare parts to the customer. 

This is possible by adding a partner function "Delivery Point" in the Spare Part item in the Service Order. The address of this partner is copied into the follow-up Purchase Requisition and Purchase Order as "Delivery Address". 

Customizing settings in Partner Determination procedure.

The Partner Function "Delivery Point" has Function Category 0017 - Delivery Point.

entry.title: Service with Advanced Execution: Process Flow 3 – Planning and Cost Estimation in Service Quotation
entry.content:
Service with Advanced Execution is a new service business process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This is based on the integration of Service with Maintenance Management.

The concept of Execution Status in Service with Advanced Execution is introduced in the following blog:

Execution Status in Service with Advanced Execution

Within Service with Advanced Execution, there are various process flows depending on the business scenario. These process flows are distinguished based on two aspects:

  • How the process starts, and which is the first transaction.
  • What is the trigger for the creation of the maintenance order and eventually the start of planning.

Process Flow 3:

This blog showcases the process flow wherein the initial transaction is the service order quotation, and the maintenance order is created from the execution order item in the service order quotation for the purpose of initial planning and cost estimation.

For example, let us assume that a particular customer equipment has suffered an extensive damage due to an industrial accident. Hence the customer is requesting for repair of this equipment and asking for a quote. The Service Manager has agreed with the customer to perform fixed price billing. However, there is no price master in this case and detailed investigation may be required to come up with a competitive quote.

In such scenarios, the service manager will request for an initial planning to come up with a rough estimate on the cost of repair. For services that demand detailed planning, a billable maintenance order is created for each item in the service quotation. The maintenance planner then performs a detailed investigation of the labor and parts required, thus arriving at a cost estimation. Once this planning data is incorporated in the service order quotation, the service manager can add an appropriate margin and provide a quote to the customer based on this detailed planning.

Creation of Service Order Quotation

A service order quotation can be created using the ‘Create Service Quotation’ App.

The following details could be filled while creating a service order quotation document:

  • Sold-to-Party
  • Organizational data such as Sales Organization and optionally Service Organization
  • Parties involved such as Service Employee Group and Employee Responsible
  • Execution Order Item with Planning Plant, Work Center, DIP Profile and Billing Relevance
  • Reference Objects

After entering all the details, save the service order quotation document. The service order quotation gets created with an execution order item with lifecycle status as ‘Open’. The lifecycle status of the execution order item can be changed to ‘In Process’ to begin with the planning phase.

In the Items assignment block, filter the items to display only the execution order items and the Send to Planning column will be displayed. To create the billable maintenance order, the Send to Planning checkbox can be selected for the execution order items which require detailed planning. The billable maintenance order is created in ‘CRTD’ (Created) status along with ‘ORAR’ (Order Approval Required) status. The execution status on the execution order item is set as ‘Execution Planning’ as soon as the maintenance order is created.

The document flow between the service order quotation and maintenance order can be viewed in Transaction History assignment block.

The details of the maintenance order connected to the execution order item can be viewed in the Maintenance Order assignment block in the item details page.

Status lifecycle in Service with Advanced Execution

                                                          Current state of an execution order item

The state diagram illustrates the status lifecycle of one execution order item along with its corresponding maintenance order in this process flow. Each block below represents a state displaying 3 different status values. The lifecycle and execution status of an execution order item along with the status of the corresponding maintenance order.

Note: The execution order item in service order quotation can’t be released or sent to customer when the execution status is ‘Execution Planning’.

Planning Completed in Maintenance Order

When the planning is completed in the maintenance order by the Maintenance Planner, the action Planning Completed is performed on the maintenance order which in turn sets the ‘ORAI’ (Order Approval in Process) status on the maintenance order. As a result, the execution status of the execution order item is set to ‘Planning Completed’.

Once the planning is completed, the cost estimates are transferred to a price condition in the service order quotation. This can be used to negotiate and finalize the service price within the service order quotation along with an appropriate margin. The finalized quotation can then be sent to the customer for approval, or, if needed, an additional planning cycle can be initiated to refine the details further.

Replanning of Execution Order Item

At this stage, the customer may request adjustments to the planning data or the terms of the quotation. In such cases, the status of the execution order item can be reset to 'In Process'. This enables another cycle of planning, including Send to Planning, Planning Completed, and Send to Customer. With this, the Send to Planning function can be used again. If the Send to Planning is selected again, the previously created billable maintenance order becomes editable, and its status changes to ‘ORRJ’ (Order Rejected). At this stage, the maintenance order cannot be released, and the service order quotation cannot be sent to the customer as the execution status of execution order item is ‘Execution Planning’.

Once the necessary adjustments are made, the maintenance order can be set again to Planning Completed and status is set to ‘ORAI’ (Order Approval in Process), the execution status of the execution order item is set to ‘Planning Completed’.

This process of planning adjustments and corrections can be repeated multiple cycles as needed, until the planning data meets the service requirements and customer satisfaction.

Send to Customer

When the service order quotation is sent to customer, the execution order item status is updated to ‘Released’.

Accept and creation of Follow-up Service Order

When the customer accepts the service order quotation, its status is updated to Completed, and the maintenance order status changes to ‘ORAP’ (Order Approved). A follow-up service order is automatically created, carrying over the reference to the existing maintenance order in the execution order item to retain the planning data.

With the maintenance order in ‘ORAP’ (Order Approved) status, the execution of the service can be started after release of the maintenance order.

Further, the regular flow and options of the service order processing follows.

Summary

The distinct feature in this process flow is the creation of maintenance order from the execution order item in a service order quotation. This helps the customer to get the cost and time estimates from the detailed planning done in the maintenance order. As we proceed with the execution of the job, the lifecycle and execution status of execution order item are in continuous alignment with the status of maintenance order at every step.

 

Further references

To know more about the execution order item and its attributes, please refer following blog:

Deep Dive: Execution Order Item and Billable Maintenance Order

entry.title: Introducing Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Monitoring in PP/DS for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Background

SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling (PP/DS) capability is used to create procurement proposals for in-house production or external procurement to cover product requirements and to optimize and plan the resource schedule and the order dates/times in detail. PP/DS helps a Production Planner to reduce the lead times, to increase on-time delivery performance, or to increase the throughput of products and reduce the stock costs.

However, how would the Production Planner know whether their production plan is really helping them achieve their goals or not?

Depending on the complexity of the business, a production planner could be dealing with hundreds of resources, thousands of products, and thousands of partners and customers. Monitoring the Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for complex production plans is critical to ensure that the production plan is feasible and is aligned with business goals.

 

Introduction

As part of the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) release 2024.Q4, a new Business Content Package for Line of Business (LoB) titled "Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling" is released. This Business Content Package includes 4 stories -

  • Order Details
  • Order Operations
  • Pegging Relationships (Order to Demand)
  • Resource Capacity

The above SAC stories need a live data connection to SAP S/4HANA 2023 system. The four Analytical Queries released as part of SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 serve as the data source for these stories

Once you have successfully created the connection between your SAC tenant and your SAP S/4HANA system, the SAC stories would display the KPIs and charts based on your data.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) stories

Order Details

The SAP PP/DS: KPI Monitoring - Order Details story has an Overview and a Details page, and displays important performance indicators about your orders, such as the:

  • number of orders
  • number of delayed orders
  • lead, processing, queue, setup, and wait time
  • number of schedule lines
  • confirmed, original, receipt, scrap, and total quantity

Figure 1: Order Details - OverviewFigure 2: Order Details - Details

This story also links to and from the Order Operations and Pegging Relationships (Order to Demand) stories.

Order Operations

The SAP PP/DS: KPI Monitoring - Order Operations story has an Overview page and displays important performance indicators about the operations of your orders, such as the:

  • number of operations
  • number of sub operations
  • gross operation duration
  • net operation duration

Figure 3: Order Operations - Overview

This story also links to and from the Order Details and Resource Capacity stories.

Pegging Relationships (Order to Demand)

The SAP PP/DS: KPI Monitoring - Pegging Relationships (Order to Demand) story has an Overview and a Details page, and displays important performance indicators about your orders and their pegging relationships, such as the:

  • service level percentage
  • maximum earliness and lateness time
  • total buffer time
  • early, on-time, delayed, and total relationships count
  • requirement quantity

Figure 4: Pegging Relationships (Order to Demand) - OverviewFigure 5: Pegging Relationships (Order to Demand) - Details

This story also links to and from the Order Details story.

Resource Capacity

The SAP PP/DS: KPI Monitoring - Resource Capacity story has an Overview and a Details page, and displays important performance indicators about your resources, such as the:

  • number of overloaded resources
  • capacity utilization
  • capacity required for setup, teardown, production, and queue

Figure 6: Resource Capacity - OverviewFigure 7: Resource Capacity - Details

This story also links to and from the Order Operations story.

 

Wrapping Up

The SAC stories for KPI Monitoring in PP/DS can be used to track and monitor the KPIs based on your current production plan. You can use these SAC stories "as is", or by fine-tuning them or by using them as reference to create your own SAC stories. 

Please feel free to share your feedback and comments.

P.S.: Refer SAP Note 3495936 - Restrictions and Implementation Recommendations for Analytics in PP/DS on SAP S/4HANA 2023 to ensure all the pre-requisites are maintained in your SAP S/4HANA system.

entry.title: Billing relevance B in execution order items in Service with Advanced Execution
entry.content:
The concept of Billing Relevance in Service with Advanced Execution is already explained in a previous blog: Billing relevance in execution order item

This blog is to showcase the Service with Advanced execution process flow with a specific type of billing relevance which is Billing via Billing Plan After Release (Key = B).

Billing via Billing Plan After Release is a billing scenario used when billing dates and billing values are to be scheduled and processed based on agreed milestones. This billing scenario can only be used for ad hoc billing. Ad hoc billing is a structured method of billing that doesn’t conform to periodic or milestone billing plan rules but instead enables you to define billing values and dates freely. You can use an ad hoc billing plan to define the dates on which billing is to occur and the value that is to be billed. For each billing date, the system generates one billing request. Based on the billing document request, you can post an invoice.

With this billing relevance, billing process can start only after release of execution order item.

Prerequisites

  1. Make the necessary settings for ad hoc billing plans in customizing under:
    Service -> Transactions -> Basic Settings -> Billing Plan -> Define Billing Plan Types.
    In the Billing Plan Usage field, choose Ad-hoc Billing. Execution Order Items support only Ad-hoc Billing and not Periodic Billing.

Define Billing Plan Types

2. The billing plan type created in the above step should be assigned to the relevant item category in the              customizing under: 
    Service -> Transactions -> Basic Settings -> Billing Plan -> Assign Billing Plan Type to Item Category.

Assign Bill Plan Type to Item Category

Process Flow

The following steps depicts the process flow for Billing via Billing Plan After Release:

1. Create a service order and add an execution order item.

Service Order with an execution order item

2. Navigate to the item overview page in Edit mode, scroll down to the Billing assignment block and change Billing Relevance to Billing via Billing Plan After Release.

Billing assignment block in an execution order item

On setting the billing relevance as described above, the Billing Plan assignment block becomes visible.

3. Click on Insert and add billing request lines with billing dates and billing values as required.

Billing Plan assignment block

You can even set a billing block if required, so that billing is not triggered unless this block is removed.

You need to make sure that you have entered billing request lines with appropriate billing values such that it adds up to the net value of the item.

Error due to mismatch in sum of BRL billing values and item net value

You can even use the Bill Remaining Net Value button to add a new entry with the remaining value filled automatically. The billing date of such a billing request line is set to the current date by default.

Billing Plan assignment block

As highlighted, there is a billing block set on the Final Payment.

4. Release the item and save the service order. Billing is possible only after the release of the execution order item.

5. To bill the Initial Advance, the first step is to create the billing document request (BDR).  This can be performed by manually scheduling a job using the report CRMS4_SERVORD_BDR_GENERATE. The job can be scheduled in the background as well. You can find the report on the SAP Easy Access menu under
Service -> Service Processes.

6. Provide the relevant inputs and execute the report. It creates BDRs for each of the valid Billing Request Lines in the relevant Service Orders.

BDR Generation Log/ Display logs for BDR Creation

You can find the BDR numbers in this log. As you can see here, there is only one BDR created which is corresponding to the Initial Advance.  The Final Payment is not processed due to the billing block.

Billing Plan assignment block after creating one BDR

7. As a next step, in Create Billing Documents app, create an invoice based on the BDR.

Create Billing Documents App

 

Invoice

The Net Value of the billing document, in general, matches the Billing Value of the BRL.

8. Open the Billing Plan assignment block in execution order item again, you can see that the invoice number and status is updated.

Billing Plan assignment block after billing one BRL

9. Open the billing document again using Manage Billing Documents app and click on Post Billing Document to release for accounting.

Manage Billing Documents App

10. Go back to the Service Order to the Billing Plan assignment block of the execution order item:

Billing Plan assignment block after posting one Invoice

The Billing Document Request and Invoice (Billing Document) is updated in the transaction history of the item as well.

Transaction History assignment block after creating one invoice

The billing details are also updated accordingly. Since only one BRL is billed, the billing status is set as ‘Partially Billed’.

Billing assignment block after creating one invoice

11. Once the job is completed by the maintenance technician, set the item to status Completed. Remove the billing block for the second BRL and perform the billing once again which will set the Billing Status to ‘Completely Billed’.

Transaction History assignment block after billing all BRLs

 

Billing assignment block after billing all BRLs

This ensures that the billing process is completed.

entry.title: Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition | 2023 FPS02 Release
entry.content:
Welcome back to my blog post series about Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition!

On the 8th October 2024, SAP has released the second “Feature Pack Stack (FPS)”, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition for the 2023 release.
For a comprehensive overview of selected innovation highlights across all lines of business, see the blog post 

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition | 2023 FPS02 Release – Part 1

In this blog post we will dive deeper into the innovations for Maintenance Management as part of the line of business Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Release 2023 FPS02. 

 

Table of Contents

Enhancements in area of Phase Model 

  • Skipping screening phase in reactive maintenance process
  • Enhanced configuration for assigning overall status profile
  • Evaluation of maintenance order before entering next subphase

Inspection Checklist Process

  • Inspection Checklist Process Enhancements
  • Integrated checklist-handling in Find Technical Objects and Find Maintenance Orders and Operations
  • New SAP Fiori app Manage Inspection Checklists

Closed Loop

  • Integrated, cross-app navigation from the Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders app and Find Maintenance Orders app through BAdI implementation
  • Cross-application navigation from maintenance-order processing to permit to work 
  • Recommendations from SAP Asset Performance Management in the SAP Fiori app “Create Maintenance Request”
  • Enhanced integration of SAP Asset Performance Management into task lists and maintenance items for SAP Fiori apps
  • Providing a catalog-based “Failure Effect” field in the SAP Fiori apps for maintenance requests
  • Enabling situation handling for maintenance task lists and maintenance plans 

Enhancements for Perform Maintenance Jobs app

  • Overview of Team assignments
  • Overview of PRT assignments
  • Overview of planned Services
  • Planned Work and Remaining Work
  • Business Catalog for Cross-App Navigation

Purchase Contract Details in Maintenance Orders and Task Lists

Improve accuracy of material costs in refurbishment process

AI-assisted Maintenance Order Recommendation

Mass change of custom fields using the “Manage Maintenance Plans” and “Manage Maintenance Items” apps

Mass Generation of Permit to Work Objects

More Information / Additional Links

 

Enhancements in area of Phase Model 

Configuring Requirements for Creating Phase-Enabled Orders - Skipping screening phase in reactive maintenance process

If you create maintenance requests and maintenance orders of a specific type, these maintenance requests and orders are processed according to phases and become part of a comprehensive end-to-end process. In this business process, a standard logic controls the prerequisites that must be fulfilled before you can create a
phase-enabled maintenance order in the Planning phase. The underlying SAP standard logic is as follows:

  • Maintenance orders of the Reactive Maintenance order type must always be based on an accepted
    maintenance request. The application logic of the order evaluates that the header notification has been accepted during the screening phase, i.e. system status APOK (Approval: OK) has been activated. In other words: You can only create a reactive maintenance order in the Planning phase if the corresponding maintenance request has the system status APOK (Approval: OK) and is in the Accepted (Request) subphase. As soon as the accepted maintenance request has been assigned to a maintenance order, it is renamed to "maintenance notification". This header notification requires a priority and a final due date.
    In the case that the header notification does not include this active system status, the reactive maintenance order will not be created.
  • Maintenance orders of the Proactive Maintenance order type do not necessarily need to be based on a
    maintenance request. They can also be created based on a maintenance plan, for example. If you base a proactive maintenance order on a maintenance request, the maintenance request does not need to have run through an approval process.

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Release 2023 FPS02, configuration and a Business Add-In (BAdI) is provided that allows you to overwrite the standard application logic:

The new Business Add-In (BAdI) BAdI: Processing of Phase-Enabled Maintenance Orders
(EAM_CREATE_PHASE_ENABLED_ORDER) and the Customizing activity Define Requirements for Creating
Phase-Enabled Orders (Customizing view V_EAM_NOTIFCNFG) allow you to determine whether maintenance
requests need to be approved before phase-enabled maintenance orders can be based on them.

With the Customizing view V_EAM_NOTIFCNFG and the BAdI method CREATE_PHASE_ENABLED_ORDER, you can determine different combinations of planning plants, notification types and order types for which you want to implement a business logic different from the SAP standard described above. On the level of the maintenance planning plant, you can determine for each combination of notification type and order type whether an accepted maintenance request is mandatory for creating the corresponding phase-enabled maintenance order. For example, you can determine that in a specific planning plant, reactive maintenance orders can be created based on maintenance requests that have not been accepted. Or you can determine that proactive maintenance orders only can be created based on accepted maintenance requests.

In the example below,

  • a reactive maintenance order of type YA01 and maintenance planning plant 1010 can be raised against a notification of type M1. 
  • the notification Type M1 has no Approval Required flag active, so that any notification of that type will not enter the screening phase at all. Hence, those notification will never have system status APOK (Approval: OK) active.
  • without this configuration, the reactive maintenance order could not have been raised against a notification of type M1.

You can access the corresponding activity in Customizing: Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> Maintenance and Service Processing -> Fiori Apps for Maintenance Processing -> General Settings -> Define Requirements for Creating Phase-Enabled Orders

Figure 1: Customizing - Change View “View for controlling the creation of phase-enabled orders”: Overview

In addition, see Customizing: Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> Maintenance and Service Processing -> Maintenance and Service Notifications -> Overview of Notification Type

Figure 2: Customizing - Change View “Partner Functions, Approval”: Details

In the case that the configuration does not fulfil your business requirements, you can implement the BAdI EAM_CREATE_PHASE_ENABLED_ORDER. If you have entered the corresponding data in the Customizing view, the BAdI is called in the following cases:

  • You create a phase-enabled maintenance order in the Create Maintenance Order app.
  • You create a phase-enabled maintenance order in the Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders app.
  • You create a phase-enabled maintenance order via the BAPI BAPI_ALM_ORDER_MAINTAIN. 

(🔙Table of Contents)

 

Enhanced configuration for assigning overall status profile

If you process your maintenance orders according to phases, your business transactions lead to specific system statuses that are set on maintenance order or order operation level. These system statuses determine
the subphases and phases to which the corresponding maintenance order or order operation is passed. The subphases with their conditions and sequence throughout the maintenance process are bundled in an
overall status profile that is assigned to an order type. We deliver a standard configuration for the Reactive
Maintenance and Proactive Maintenance process.

Up to now, you could only specify the overall status profile depending on the order type.

Figure 3: Prior 2023 FPS02 release: Customizing view - Assign Overall Status Profile to Order Type

However, you might need to modify this standard configuration slightly depending on your various
maintenance planning plants. With the 2023 FPS02 release, you can also specify the valid overall status profiles depending on a combination of order type and maintenance planning plant.

Figure 4: Starting with 2023 FPS02 release: Customizing view - Assign Overall Status Profile to Order Type

You can access the corresponding activity in Customizing: Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> Maintenance and Service Processing -> Fiori Apps for Maintenance Processing -> General Settings -> Configure Overall Status Profiles

Both screenshots shown above have the same consequence, all maintenance orders of type YA01 regardless of their maintenance planning plant will be assigned to the same overall status profile PMSP1.

Note:

  • Following restriction note 3365558, the configuration of the phase model is delivered by SAP and customers are not allowed to modify this configuration.
  • Future enhancements might remove some restrictions, so that customers might have several overall status profiles assigned to different combinations of maintenance planning plant and order types.

(🔙Table of Contents)

 

Evaluation of maintenance order before entering next subphase

A phase-model enabled maintenance order is assigned to an overall status profile which defines the conditions (using system statuses) determining each subphase of the order header and operation.

The new Business Add-In (BAdI) BAdI: Evaluation of Maintenance Order Data Including Buffer
(EAMS_EVAL_ORDER_DETAILS) allows you to evaluate data of phase-enabled maintenance orders each time
the maintenance orders are created or passed to a new subphase. This enables customers to introduce their custom logic to prevent the transition of the maintenance order into the next subphase.

As an example, a maintenance order has been released and the execution of this business transaction would determine a new subphase so that this order would be placed in the preparation phase.
Due to custom logic implemented in that BADI and the actual data of that maintenance order, the execution of this business transaction can be blocked so that this order remains in the current phase/subphase.

The BAdI reads maintenance order data from the buffer before the order data is saved on the database. You can evaluate this maintenance order data and implement warning or error messages that prevent the system from saving the order changes from the buffer to the data base. Note that you cannot use this BAdI to directly change any order data.
With this BAdI, you can evaluate and compare the following maintenance order data:

  • Order header data
  • Order operation data
  • Order operation component data
  • Data of the order object list
  • Phase control codes set on order header and on order operation level

This BAdI is called if you process phase-enabled maintenance orders

  • on the SAP Web User Interface for Plant Maintenance (PM)
  • in SAP Fiori apps
  • via the BAPI BAPI_ALM_ORDER_MAINTAIN
  • via the RAP-based API Maintenance Order (Version 2)

(🔙Table of Contents)

 

Inspection Checklist Process

For using the inspection checklist process

  • the business function LOG_EAM_CHECKLIST must be activated,
  • the checklist functionality must be enabled.

Check out also the Expert Talk: Inspection Checklist in Maintenance Management – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

We launched the inspection checklist solution first with the 2021 release and enhanced it with 2023 FPS01 release. What's new with FPS02?

Inspection Checklist Process Enhancements

In this chapter the description of each enhancement starts with a figure about the Phase Model for the Maintenance Process to provide you the context for phase-enabled orders. 

Maintenance Order: New Form Template for the Output Management

Figure 5: Phase Model for the Maintenance Process

With this feature, you can enhance a maintenance order by a new output type (CHECKLIST_REPORT for application object type MAINTENANCE_ORDER) and a new form and email template (EAM_MJP_CHECKLIST_REPORT / EAM_MJP_CL_DFLT_TMPL) . This enhancement is supported both by the classic SAP GUI as well as by WebDynpro.

Figure 6: 'Change Maintenance Order' app - new Output Type and Form Template

The new form template is an enhanced version of the old form with the following improvements:

  1. Barcode information is available for the checklist and is printed.
  2. Information on order and selected operations are printed.
  3. Checklist type of the operation is printed.
  4. Control indicator gets printed:
    + Required characteristic
    + Optional characteristic
    + After accepted characteristic
    + After rejected characteristic
    + Calculated characteristic (informational)
  5. Inspection characteristic and control indicator is printed together to avoid single lines
    at the end of a page

Figure 7: Maintenance Order: New Form Template for the Output Management

Note:

  • Deactivated and canceled checklists are not printed.
  • Inspection characteristics with the control indicators Do Not Print and No Charc Recording are not printed.
  • The fom template is translated.

 

Work Pack: New Form Template for Output Management

Figure 8: Phase Model for the Maintenance Process

With this feature, you can enhance the standard inspection checklist process for Work Pack Creation or Print in the Find Maintenance Order (F2175) and Find Maintenance Order and Operation (F2173) apps. With the new form and email template (PM_ORD_CHECKLIST_REPORT / EAM_MAINT_ORD_CL_DFLT_TMPL), the new output type (CHECKLIST_REPORT for application object type MAINTENANCE_JOB_PACKAGE), and the new grouping criterion (Operation Checklist Type), you can bundle job packs based on the checklist type functionality of order operations.

Figure 9: 'Find Maintenance Order' app - New grouping attribute 'Operation Checklist Type'

Figure 10: Work Pack - new Output Typ, Form Template, and Grouping Attribute

The new form template is an enhanced version of the old form with the following improvements (find the numbering also on the screenshot below):

  1. Barcode information is available for the checklist and is printed.
  2. Work pack/job pack information is printed.
  3. Checklist type of the operation is printed.
  4. Control indicator gets printed:
    + Required characteristic
    + Optional characteristic
    + After accepted characteristic
    + After rejected characteristic
    + Calculated characteristic (informational)
  5. Inspection characteristic and control indicator is printed together to avoid single lines
    at the end of a page

Figure 11: Work Pack / Job Pack - New Form Template for the Output Management

Note:

  • Deactivated and canceled checklists are not printed.
  • Inspection characteristics with the control indicators Do Not Print and No Charc Recording are not printed.
  • The fom template is translated.

 

Perform Maintenance Job app (F5104A)

Figure 12: Phase Model for the Maintenance Process

With the Perform Maintenance Job app you can now create follow-on notifications for inspection checklists by selecting the new button (see red dotted line in the screenshot below) in the checklists table of a job. The popover that is displayed when you select the status bar in the checklists table now shows additional details, including the usage decision valuation of the relevant inspection lot (see green dotted line). You can also display the usage decision valuation in the results table (see yellow dotted line) to inform the technician, if the inspection lot is still open for results recording.

Figure 13: 'Perform Maintenance Job' app enhancements

The Create Follow-On Notification uses the Notification Type from the Configure Checklists for Maintenance Order Types and Planning Plants activity and the technical object from the inspection lot.  A reference to the notification in the document flow is created.

Figure 14: 'Configure Checklists for Maintenance Order Types and Planning Plants' activity

Note: The Create Follow-On Notification is disabled, if the inspection lot is deactivated or has a recorded usage decision.

 

Final Time Confirmation: New system check of operations with checklists

New customizing flag Check Results Recording Status at Final Confirmation is available in customizing activity Configure Checklists for Maintenance Order Types and Planning Plants.

Figure 15: Customizing activity 'Configure Checklists for Maintenance Order Types and Planning Plants'

When the flag is set then the system checks if results have been recorded for all required or processed inspection characteristics. This check gets bypassed for deactivated, canceled or usage decision valuated inspection lots.

Final time confirmation check failed in IW41 transaction:

Figure 16: Final time confirmation check failed in IW41 transaction

Final time confirmation check failed in Perform Maintenance Jobs app:

Figure 17: Final time confirmation check failed in 'Perform Maintenance Jobs' app

 

Inspection Lot Usage Decision: New Follow-Up Actions

Figure 18: Phase Model for the Maintenance Process

With the new Follow-Up Actions you can enable the system to automatically change the system status of a technical object during checklist completion. For this purpose, three new function modules are available that you can specify as follow-up actions for usage decisions in Customizing. The function modules either set or reset the new technical object system status FLCO (Faulty Condition).

The following new function modules are available in function group EAM_CL_FOL:

  • EAM_CL_SET_TOBJ_STATUS_FLCO (Checklists Follow-Up Action: Set FLCO status on technical object)
    This follow-up action can be used to set the new technical object status FLCO independently of the inspection lot usage decision valuation:
    - The FLCO status becomes active, if it is not active.
    - You can use this follow-up action for example to accept an inspection lot based on visual characteristics, but the technical object has a technical faulty condition.
  • EAM_CL_RES_TOBJ_STATUS_FLCO (Checklists Follow-Up Action: Reset FLCO status on technical object)
    This follow-up action can be used to reset the new technical object status FLCO independently of the inspection lot usage decision valuation:
    - The FLCO status becomes inactive, if it is not inactive.
    - You can use this follow-up action for example to reject an inspection lot based on visual characteristics, but the technical condition is still valid.
  • EAM_CL_CHG_TOBJ_STATUS_FLCO (Checklists Follow-Up Action: Set or reset FLCO on tech obj based on UD) 
    This follow-up action can be used to dynamically set or remove the system status FLCO for the technical object of an inspection lot based on a predefined logic that considers the inspection lot valuation: A Rejected usage decision sets the status FLCO on the technical object. An Accepted usage decision removes the status FLCO from the technical object.

Note that the system status FLCO is informational in nature and does not trigger any business transactions on its own. However, you can control the execution of specific business transactions as a consequence of this status by defining custom user statuses that react to the setting and resetting of this status.

Integrated, cross-app navigation from the Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders app and Find Maintenance Orders app through BAdI implementation

The new Business Add-In (BAdI) Cross-App Navigation Control for Fiori Applications (EAM_CROSS_APP_NAV_CONTROL) allows you to display custom cross-app navigation links in the following SAP Fiori apps:

  • Find Maintenance Orders (F2175)
  • Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders (F4604)

The custom cross-app navigation allows you to open selected items in other Fiori apps, including apps that are
not part of SAP S/4HANA.

When you create an implementation, the additional quick action Open In becomes available in the table toolbar of the app. Business users can use this quick action to display a dropdown menu with links to the target applications that you have defined in the implementation.
In your implementation, you can define relevant target applications, link texts, and system behavior of the
cross-app navigation according to your requirements. If a target application is not included in the business
catalog assignment of a user, the respective link will not be visible.

The BAdI is available for Key User Extensibility in the Custom Logic app for for business context:

  • Asset Management: Maintenance Order
  • Maintenance Notification

If you have set up the integration of SAP S/4HANA Asset Management with SAP Asset Performance

object and the failure mode. With this feature, you can get recommendations for the task list in the Create
Maintenance Request (F1511A) app.

The next figure shows the integration scenario:

  1. Initiate Work Request
  2. Technical Object and/or Failure Data
  3. Propose Task Lists based on implemented Recommendations & addressed Failure Data
  4. Re-/Assignment of Task List based on  APM recommended Task List or S/4HANA value help
  5. Optional usage of the processing context emergency work will create an order in the execution phase with the exploded assigned task list

If you request maintenance for a specific technical object in the Create Maintenance Request app, you can

The next screenshot shows the Create Maintenance Request app with the following steps:

  1. The Task List field will be enabled, if the user has the authorization for the Emergency Work processing context (Authorization check against object I_EXCP_MN with field name ACTVT = 03 (processing context))

By the way: The button was renamed from Recommended Task List to Get Recommended Task Lists in Screen Maintenance Requests (F4072) app.
  3. Displaying additional information to support an easier task list choice.

    - Technical Object

Providing a catalog-based “Failure Effect” field in the SAP Fiori apps for maintenance requests

Technicians can now record failure effects when processing maintenance requests in SAP Fiori. While the failure mode describes the way in which a technical object fails to perform a required function, the failure effect describes the evidence or the symptom that shows that a failure has occurred.
The failure effect is part of the failure data hierarchy in SAP Asset Performance Management and is now introduced in the apps for processing maintenance requests in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management. Several apps have now been enhanced with a new field, allowing you to capture this information in your maintenance
requests.
Technicians can report the failure effect while creating a maintenance request by selecting a suitable code from the catalog profile of the technical object or notification type. As a supervisor, you can change the failure effect after inspecting the situation on site. The specified failure effect is visible for maintenance technicians while performing the repair work.
This feature improves the quality of data that is recorded during failure detection and maintenance execution to facilitate reliable failure analytics and the development of maintenance strategies based on methodologies such as Reliability-Centered Maintenance (RCM) or Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA). It also further aligns the failure data model in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management with technical standards such as IEC 60300 and IEC 60812.

Customizing of catalog-based Failure Effect: Catalog Profile and Catalog

The figure below shows the customizing steps of catalog-based failure effect:

  3. Used catalog for the failure effect field during maintenance processing

Enabling situation handling for maintenance task lists and maintenance plans 

The extended framework of Situation Handling supports you in creating custom situations. Two kinds of situations are supported: object-based and message-based situations.
The following figure gives you an overview of the process flow for custom object-based situations, including the apps and roles that are involved:

Figure 48: Process Flow for Custom Object-Based Situations

For more information, see Situation Handling - Extended Framework.

Situation Handling now delivers the following situation templates for Maintenance Management:

  • Maintenance Plan (EAM_MAINTENANCE_PLAN) - This situation template allows you to create situation
    types that can trigger situations when a maintenance plan is created or changed. From the situation
    instance details, you can navigate to the related app Manage Maintenance Plans to view details of the plan.
  • Maintenance Item (EAM_MAINTENANCE_ITEM) - This situation template allows you to create situation
    types that can trigger situations when a maintenance item is created, assigned, or changed. From the
    situation instance details, you can navigate to the related app Manage Maintenance Items to view details of the item.
  • Maintenance Task List (EAM_MAINT_TASK_LIST) - This situation template allows you to create situation
    types that can trigger situations when a maintenance task list is created or changed. From the situation
    instance details, you can navigate to the related app Find Maintenance Task List to view details of the task
    list.

Based on the situation templates, you can create situation types in the Manage Situation Types - Extended app. In a situation type, you can define the conditions and rules based on which situation instances are created to notify you when a situation occurs. Choose a situation instance to view details of the situation. You are taken to the My Situations - Extended app. You can choose the related app to view details of the business object.

The figure below shows an example use case in the Manage Situation Types - Extended app with the following parameters:

  • Situation object:     EAM_MAINT_ITEM
  • Situation scenario:  EAM_MAINTENANCE_ITEM_PROCESSING
  • Situation template:  EAM_MAINTENANCE_ITEM
